tasks.title: Insert EDA section into Rmd
tasks.description: Add new '# Exploratory Data Analysis' section between Data/Methods and Statistical Methods. Load data_validated.rds ($data tibble), join with prompt_sample_25k.csv for source/target_group. Create 4 new echo=TRUE chunks: (1) load-eda: readRDS + join + str(), (2) eda-density: ggplot density of tox_score_base by family, (3) eda-prompt-counts: bar chart or kable of RTP vs ToxiGen counts with 13 target_group breakdown, (4) eda-base-rates: side-by-side bar chart of tox_binary_base mean per family. Add casual intro paragraph.
tasks.context:
## Spec Context
Insert between Data/Methods (currently line 43) and Statistical Methods (currently line 55). Data loading: `validated <- readRDS("../analysis/data_validated.rds"); df <- validated$data` — this is a tibble with 75000 rows x 25 cols. Key cols: prompt_id, family (qwen3/llama31/mistral), tox_score_base (numeric), tox_binary_base (0/1). Join prompt metadata: `read_csv("../data/processed/prompt_sample_25k.csv")` has prompt_id, source (rtp/toxigen), target_group (13 groups or NA for RTP). All new chunks must use echo=TRUE. Use ggplot2 (already loaded). Casual tone: 'Before running the tests, let's get a feel for what the data actually look like.'

## Dependencies
None — first task. Existing setup chunk loads tidyverse, ggplot2, knitr, patchwork.

## Data Columns (RDS $data)
prompt_id, family, prompt_text, base_completion, aligned_completion, tox_score_base, tox_score_aligned, tox_binary_base, tox_binary_aligned, scorer, threshold

## Prompt CSV Columns
prompt_id, source, text, toxicity_source, target_group, stratum

## ToxiGen Groups
asian, black, chinese, jewish, latino, lgbtq, mental_dis, mexican, middle_east, muslim, native_american, physical_dis, women

tasks.title: Insert Assumption Checks section into Rmd
tasks.description: Add new '# Assumption Checks' section immediately before '# Results'. Create 3 subsections with echo=TRUE chunks: (2a) McNemar: compute discordant cell counts b and c from raw paired data per family, print b+c values, note >25 rule of thumb. (2b) Wilcoxon: compute d_i = tox_score_base - tox_score_aligned per family, plot density/histogram of d_i, comment on symmetry. (2c) Cochran: validate binary coding with table(), count NAs, confirm strict 0/1. Each subsection: one short paragraph + one code chunk.
tasks.context:
## Spec Context
Insert immediately before '# Results' section. Reuse the `df` object already loaded in TASK-001's load-eda chunk (75000-row tibble with tox_score_base, tox_score_aligned, tox_binary_base, tox_binary_aligned, family).

### 2a McNemar
Discordant pairs: b = (tox_binary_base==1 & tox_binary_aligned==0), c = (tox_binary_base==0 & tox_binary_aligned==1). Compute per family. b+c must be > 25 for chi-squared approximation.

### 2b Wilcoxon
d_i = tox_score_base - tox_score_aligned. Plot density per family. Key assumption: distribution of differences roughly symmetric.

### 2c Cochran
Binary response = 'alignment success' (base toxic -> aligned non-toxic). Confirm 0/1 only, no NAs. Use table() for validation.

## Dependencies
TASK-001 loads `df` into knitr environment. Families: qwen3, llama31, mistral.

## Tone
Casual, short paragraphs. E.g., 'Before we trust the McNemar results, let's make sure the assumptions hold.'

tasks.title: Expose existing test code with echo=TRUE
tasks.description: Add echo=TRUE chunk option to existing {r table1} and {r table2} chunks. Create a new {r cochran-q-display, echo=TRUE} chunk that computes/displays Cochran's Q results (currently inline prose only). Do NOT change global echo=FALSE in setup. Do NOT alter any computed values.
tasks.context:
## Spec Context
Current state: global setup has echo=FALSE. Three results chunks are invisible.

Changes needed:
- `{r table1}` -> `{r table1, echo=TRUE}` (McNemar display, ~line 79 originally)
- `{r table2}` -> `{r table2, echo=TRUE}` (Wilcoxon display, ~line 100 originally)
- New `{r cochran-q-display, echo=TRUE}` chunk for Cochran's Q (currently Q=442.12, df=2, p=9.9e-97 is inline prose only)

The new Cochran chunk should display the cochran_results data already loaded by the load-data chunk. Format with kable().

Do NOT change the global echo=FALSE in setup chunk.

## Dependencies
TASK-002 must complete first (line numbers shifted by EDA and Assumption insertions).

tasks.title: Rewrite prose in casual grad-student tone
tasks.description: Rewrite all formal/stiff connective prose throughout the Rmd in a casual grad-student voice. Affected sections: Introduction, Data and Methods, Statistical Methods, Discussion, plus new EDA and Assumption Checks prose. Keep statistical terminology precise -- only soften 'connective tissue'. Do NOT change: statistical method descriptions, numeric values, p-values, effect sizes, CI ranges, figure/table captions, References section.

verification.command: ! grep -qi 'empirical evidence quantifying\|Despite widespread adoption' docs/COMP4441-FinalProject-SafetyAlignmentEffectiveness-LLM-RockLambros.Rmd && echo 'PASS: formal phrases removed'

tasks.context:
## Spec Context
Style guide examples:
- 'empirical evidence quantifying alignment effectiveness across model families remains limited' -> 'but we don't really have solid numbers on how well this works across different model families'
- 'This study asks:' -> 'The main question I'm trying to answer here is:'
- 'Despite widespread adoption' -> 'Even though everyone's using these techniques'
- Sound like a grad student writing a class paper, not a journal submission

Constraints:
- Do NOT change statistical method descriptions (McNemar's formula, Wilcoxon, Cochran's Q definitions)
- Do NOT change any numeric values, p-values, effect sizes, or CI ranges
- Do NOT change figure captions or table captions
- Do NOT alter the References section
- Sections affected: Introduction, Data and Methods, Statistical Methods, Discussion, and new EDA/Assumptions prose

## Dependencies
TASK-003 must complete. All structural changes done; this is final content pass before knit test.

tasks.title: Validate Rmd knits without errors
tasks.description: Knit the Rmd to both word_document and pdf_document. Verify: (1) no knit errors, (2) EDA section appears with plots, (3) Assumption Checks section appears, (4) R code is visible in table1/table2/cochran-q-display chunks, (5) existing figures/tables still render correctly, (6) no broken cross-references.

verification.command: cd docs && Rscript -e "rmarkdown::render('COMP4441-FinalProject-SafetyAlignmentEffectiveness-LLM-RockLambros.Rmd', output_format='word_document', quiet=TRUE)" && echo 'KNIT SUCCESS'

tasks.context:

2. EDA contains density plot, prompt counts (13 ToxiGen groups), base rates
3. Assumption Checks before Results
4. All new + modified chunks have echo=TRUE

conflict_matrix.description: All tasks modify the same Rmd file sequentially. No parallel execution possible.
